texto: Time — how it expands to fill the spaces you create; how it makes meagre experiences seem never-ending. Whenever he heard people talk about the ravages of time, about how it robbed and deprived, Justin always smiled; because for him, time was an accomplice, plugging the gaps and fleshing out morsels of memory so he would have something substantial to hang on to. That way, however little he had seen or felt, he would always feel as if he had more: a life far richer than the truth.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Tash Aw: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Time — how it expands to fill the spaces you create; how it makes meagre experiences seem never-e…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, memory, time pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e time.
